chat.confirmInterrupt: A conversation is in progress. Creating a new session will interrupt it. Continue?

chat.inputPlaceholderEnter: @reference files, #invoke agents, !insert prompts, Enter to send
chat.sdkNotInstalled: {{provider}} SDK is not installed. Please install the SDK to start chatting.

settings.title: Settings

basic.title: Basic Configuration
basic.description: Configure page theme, language and Node.js environment

theme.label: Theme
theme.lightDesc: Fresh and bright, suitable for daytime use

nodePath.label: Node.js Path
nodePath.hintText: to get the actual Node.js executable path. Leave empty to auto-detect.
nodePath.versionTooLow: Node.js version is too low. The plugin requires v{{minVersion}} or higher to run properly

workingDirectory.label: Working Directory
workingDirectory.placeholder: e.g. project1 or leave empty to use project root
workingDirectory.hint: Specify the directory where Claude works. Can use relative path (relative to project root) or absolute path. Leave empty to use project root.

editorFont.label: Font (follows editor font)
editorFont.hint: Not editable here. To modify, go to IDEA Settings → Editor → Font (restart IDEA for plugin font to take effect)

streaming.label: Streaming
streaming.hint: When enabled, AI responses will appear character by character in real-time, instead of waiting for the complete response. May consume more resources.

autoOpenFile.label: Send Opened File Path
autoOpenFile.hint: When enabled, selecting a file in the project tree will automatically send its file path to AI (note: only the file path is sent, not the file content). When disabled, manual action is required.

diffExpanded.label: Expand Diffs by Default
diffExpanded.hint: When enabled, code diffs in chat will be expanded by default. When disabled, diffs start collapsed and can be expanded by clicking.

sendShortcut.label: Send Shortcut
sendShortcut.enterDesc: Press Enter to send message, Shift+Enter for new line
sendShortcut.cmdEnterDesc: Press ⌘/Ctrl+Enter to send message, Enter for new line

chatBgColor.label: Chat Background
chatBgColor.hint: Customize the chat area background color. Leave empty to follow the theme default.

soundNotification.label: Task Completion Sound
soundNotification.hint: When enabled, a notification sound will play when AI completes a task, so you'll know even when away from the screen.
soundNotification.onlyWhenUnfocused: Play only when IDE is not focused
soundNotification.onlyWhenUnfocusedHint: When enabled, the sound will only play when the IDE window is not in focus.
soundNotification.customSoundPlaceholder: Select or enter audio file path

userMsgColor.label: User Message Color
userMsgColor.hint: Customize the background color of your messages. Leave empty to follow the theme default.

settings.providersDesc: Manage Claude and Codex API provider configurations and switch between different API service providers

pluginModels.title: Custom Models
pluginModels.description: Custom models added here will be visible under all providers, shown at the top of the model selector

settings.dependenciesDesc: Manage AI SDK dependency installations and updates

dependency.title: SDK Dependency Management
dependency.description: Manage AI SDK dependencies. Install the required SDK before first use.
dependency.installPolicyTip: Installation issues? Copy the error to a terminal CLI AI to resolve
dependency.claudeSdkDescription: Required for Claude AI features. Includes Claude Code SDK and related dependencies.
dependency.codexSdkDescription: Required for Codex AI features. Includes OpenAI Codex SDK.
dependency.nodeNotConfigured: Node.js is not configured. Please set the Node.js path in Basic Settings first.

settings.usageDesc: View your Token consumption, cost statistics and usage trend analysis
settings.mcpDesc: Configure and manage Model Context Protocol servers
settings.permissionsDesc: Manage Claude Code file access and operation permissions
settings.codexPermissionsDesc: Manage Codex permissions and sandbox policies

permissionsPanel.codexSandboxDesc: Control system access boundaries when Codex executes commands. Defaults to danger-full-access. Switch to workspace-write for stricter sandboxing.
permissionsPanel.workspaceWriteDesc: Read/write is limited to the workspace. System-level operations are restricted for safety.
permissionsPanel.fullAccessDesc: Allows higher system access, suitable for commands like adb or docker that require extra permissions.
permissionsPanel.hint: This setting only affects Codex process sandboxing and does not change Claude Code permission behavior.
permissionsPanel.claudeTip: Claude Code approval mode is configured from the chat input mode selector.

commit.title: Commit AI
commit.description: Configure AI-generated Git commit message behavior

prompt.label: Additional Prompt
prompt.placeholder: Add your extra requirements here, e.g., generate commit in English, include issue number, etc...
prompt.hint: The plugin has built-in CCG Commits specification prompts. Content you add here will be used as additional requirements, and AI will prioritize your instructions.

codeReview.label: Code Review AI Additional Prompt
codeReview.placeholder: This feature is under development, stay tuned...
codeReview.hint: Code Review AI will help you automatically review code, identify potential issues, and provide improvement suggestions. You can configure review preferences and focus areas here.

historyCompletion.label: History Input Completion
historyCompletion.hint: When enabled, the input box will auto-complete based on input history. Press Tab to accept the suggestion.

editor.importanceHint: Higher importance means higher priority in auto-completion

settings.codexFeatureUnavailable: This settings section is not available in Codex yet. Please switch to Claude Code.

settings.githubDesc: This project is open source on GitHub. Star us to show your support!
settings.githubCopied: Repository URL copied to clipboard. Please visit it in your browser.
settings.githubCopyFailed: Failed to copy. Please copy manually.
settings.sponsorDesc: If this project helps you, consider sponsoring to support ongoing maintenance!

provider.noConfigDescription: No readable API key or endpoint was found in the authorized local Claude configuration.
provider.deleteMessage: Are you sure you want to delete provider "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone.
provider.convertMessage: After conversion, the connection with the import source will be disconnected. This action is irreversible.
provider.convertSuccess: Conversion successful, new ID generated and disconnected
provider.featureComingSoon: Feature not yet implemented, coming soon
provider.editCcSwitchWarning: You are editing a cc-switch configuration. Editing will not update cc-switch, and imports may overwrite your changes. Consider converting to local configuration before editing.
provider.convertConfirmMessage: Convert cc-switch configuration "{{name}}" to plugin configuration?
provider.convertDetailMessage: After conversion, the ID connection with cc-switch will be disconnected, and future imports won't overwrite this configuration.
provider.configHiddenTitle: Local Claude configuration is hidden by default
provider.configHiddenDescription: To protect local credentials and settings, the plugin will not read ~/.claude/settings.json until you explicitly authorize a one-time load.
provider.officialRouteNotice: Requests are configured to go directly to the official Anthropic API.
provider.proxyRouteNotice: Requests are routed through a third-party or proxy endpoint instead of directly to the official Anthropic API.
provider.localProviderAuthorizeMessage: This will allow the plugin to read ~/.claude/settings.json for Claude requests.
provider.localProviderAuthorizeDetail: You can revoke this authorization at any time. The plugin will not modify your local file.
provider.localProviderDisableMessage: This will stop using ~/.claude/settings.json and leave Claude with no active provider until you explicitly enable another one.
provider.localProviderHelp:
When using local provider mode:
• The plugin will not modify your ~/.claude/settings.json file
• You need to manually manage your configurations
• Make sure the file exists and contains valid JSON
• Suitable for advanced users who prefer manual control
provider.cliLoginProviderDescription: Reuse your existing Claude CLI login state completed in the terminal
provider.cliLoginAuthorizeMessage: This will allow the plugin to use your Claude CLI login session for API requests. You must have already logged in via the Claude CLI in your terminal.
provider.cliLoginAuthorizeDetail: The plugin delegates authentication to the Claude SDK's native OAuth flow. You can revoke this authorization at any time.
provider.cliLoginDisableMessage: This will stop using CLI login state and leave Claude with no active provider until you explicitly enable another one.

subscriptionTutorial.dialogTitle: How to use a subscription account

claude.title: How to use Claude Code Subscription Account
claude.item1: Log in to your Claude Code subscription account via claude code CLI in the terminal, then you can chat normally in the terminal
claude.item2: Make sure the ~/.claude/settings.json file and .env file are empty
claude.item3: Restart IDEA, and you can chat normally
claude.note1: If you have done the above but the plugin still does not respond, try setting ~/.claude/settings.json .env to:
claude.issuePrefix: If it still doesn't work, please report an issue on GitHub:

codex.title: Codex Subscription Account Setup
codex.item1: Run codex login in the terminal Codex CLI and log in successfully, then you can chat in the terminal
codex.issuePrefix: If it still doesn't work, please report an issue on GitHub:

skills.title: Skills
skills.importHint: Click + button to import Skill file or folder
skills.deleteMessage:
Are you sure you want to delete {{scope}} Skill "{{name}}"?

This action cannot be undone.

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: Skills are dynamically loaded instruction sets, scripts, and resource folders that enhance Claude's performance for specific tasks. Skills teach Claude how to complete particular tasks in a repeatable way, such as creating documents using company brand guidelines, analyzing data following organization-specific workflows, or automating personal tasks.

structure.title: Skill Structure
structure.description: A Skill is a folder containing a SKILL.md file:
structure.example:
my-skill/
├── SKILL.md          # Required: skill definition file
├── templates/        # Optional: template files
└── references/       # Optional: reference materials

format.title: SKILL.md Format
format.description: SKILL.md files use YAML frontmatter + Markdown format:
format.example:
---
name: my-skill-name
description: Skill description and when to use
---

# Skill Instructions

Detailed instruction content...
format.hint: name and description are required fields. Optional fields include license, allowed-tools, metadata

tips.title: Usage Tips
tips.item1: Ensure Skill directory contains a valid SKILL.md file
tips.item2: Skill names must use lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens (hyphen-case)
tips.item3: Skills automatically take effect in Claude sessions after loading
tips.item4: Control individual Skill status with enable/disable toggles
tips.item5: Mention Skill name in chat to use it, e.g., "Use the pdf skill to extract form fields"

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: Codex Skills are instruction sets stored in .agents/skills/ directories that enhance Codex's performance for specific tasks. Invoke them in chat using the $ prefix.

structure.title: Skill Structure
structure.description: A Codex Skill is a folder containing a SKILL.md file:
structure.example:
.agents/skills/
└── my-skill/
    ├── SKILL.md          # Required: skill definition file
    ├── templates/        # Optional: template files
    └── references/       # Optional: reference materials

format.title: SKILL.md Format
format.description: SKILL.md files use YAML frontmatter + Markdown format:
format.example:
---
name: my-skill-name
description: Skill description and when to use
---

# Skill Instructions

Detailed instruction content...
format.hint: name and description are required fields

tips.title: Usage Tips
tips.item1: Ensure Skill directory contains a valid SKILL.md file
tips.item2: Skill names must use lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens
tips.item3: Type $ prefix in chat to trigger Skill autocomplete
tips.item4: Control individual Skill status with enable/disable toggles

gpt53codex.label: gpt-5.3-codex
gpt53codex.description: Latest frontier agentic coding model with enhanced capabilities.

gpt54.label: gpt-5.4
gpt54.description: Latest frontier model with enhanced capabilities.

gpt51codexMax.label: gpt-5.1-codex-max
gpt51codexMax.description: Codex-optimized flagship for deep and fast reasoning.

gpt51codexMini.label: gpt-5.1-codex-mini
gpt51codexMini.description: Optimized for codex. Cheaper, faster, but less capable.

default.label: Default Mode
default.description: Requires manual confirmation for each operation, suitable for cautious use

plan.label: Plan Mode
plan.description: Uses only read-only tools, generates plan for user approval

acceptEdits.label: Agent Mode
acceptEdits.description: Automatically accept file creation/editing, reducing confirmation steps

bypassPermissions.label: Auto Mode
bypassPermissions.description: Fully automated, bypassing all permission checks [Use with caution]

default.label: Suggest (approval)
default.tooltip: Codex approval_policy=untrusted – prompts before editing files or running shell commands.
default.description: Safest option. Every write or command requires your approval.

plan.label: Plan Mode
plan.description: Uses only read-only tools, generates plan for user approval

acceptEdits.label: Auto Edit
acceptEdits.tooltip: Codex approval_policy=on-request – asks for approval when needed
acceptEdits.description: Balanced option. Allows normal editing flow while still requiring approvals when needed.

bypassPermissions.label: Full Auto
bypassPermissions.tooltip: Codex approval_policy=never – runs commands and writes files without prompting (workspace sandbox still enforced).
bypassPermissions.description: Hands-off mode. Codex executes edits and shell commands immediately.

promptEnhancer.title: Prompt Enhancer
promptEnhancer.tooltipFull: Rewrite prompt to provide more context and improve agent responses

fileContext.enableDescription: When enabled, the currently opened file path in the editor will be sent to AI. Note: only the file path is transmitted, not the file content.

rewind.title: Rewind Files to Previous State
rewind.tooltipFull: Restore files to their state at this message
rewind.noRewindableMessages: No rewindable messages in this session
rewind.willRestore: Will restore files to their state at this message
rewind.changesLost: Changes made after this point will be lost
rewind.notAvailable: Rewind not available for this session

errorBoundary.title: Something went wrong
errorBoundary.description: The application encountered an unexpected error. Please copy the error details below and share them with the developer for troubleshooting.
errorBoundary.cause1: Node.js is not installed or not configured correctly
errorBoundary.helpText: If the problem persists, please copy the error information and submit it as an issue on GitHub, or share it in the community group.

aiBridge.claudeCodeInterrupted: Claude Code was interrupted (possibly response timeout or user cancellation):
aiBridge.notConfiguredEmpty: Not configured (value is empty or missing)
aiBridge.configTip: Tip: CLI can read from environment variables or settings.json; this plugin only supports reading from settings.json to avoid issues. You can configure it in the plugin's top-right Settings > Provider Management
aiBridge.apiErrorCauses:
Possible causes:
1. API Key is not configured correctly
2. Third-party proxy service configuration issue
3. Please check the configuration in ~/.claude/settings.json
aiBridge.codexAuthErrorChecks:
Please check the following:
1. Is the Codex API Key in plugin settings correct
2. Does the API Key have sufficient permissions
3. If using a custom Base URL, please confirm the address is correct
aiBridge.codexAuthTip: Tip: Codex requires a valid OpenAI API Key
aiBridge.codexNetworkErrorChecks:
Please check:
1. Is the network connection working
2. If using a proxy, please confirm proxy configuration
3. Is the firewall blocking the connection
aiBridge.codexErrorCheck: Please check network connection and Codex configuration
aiBridge.codexThinkingHint: Codex did not return reasoning items. If you still cannot see the thinking process, please refer to docs/codex/docs/config.md for hide_agent_reasoning/show_raw_agent_reasoning settings, and ensure your OpenAI account has been verified.

error.providerNotConfiguredDesc: No AI provider is configured yet, or access to the local configuration has not been authorized. Please complete setup in Provider Management settings before starting a conversation.
